title: Well-done: k_panic on lora_recv buffer overflow
description: lora_l2.c:197-201 panics when lora_recv returns more than the buffer size. The comment explains why continuing would be worse: stack corruption has ALREADY occurred, the return address may be corrupted, and continuing risks exploiting the corrupted state. This is the correct defensive posture for a driver bug that already caused memory corruption. Don't soften this to a LOG_ERR + continue.
